children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this part is to direct actions to prohibit participation in any foreign talent recruitment program by personnel of Federal research agencies and to prohibit participation in a malign foreign talent recruitment program by covered individuals involved with research and development awards from those agencies.

prohibit all personnel of each Federal research agency, including Federal employees, contract employees, independent contractors, individuals serving under the Intergovernmental Personnel Act of 1970 ( et seq ), Visiting Scientist, Engineering, and Educator appointments, and special government employees other than peer reviewers, from participating in a foreign talent recruitment program;42 U.S.C. 47011So in original. Probably should be followed by a period.1

as part of the requirements under , require covered individuals to disclose if such individuals are a party to a foreign talent recruitment program contract, agreement, or other arrangement;section 6605 of this title
prohibit research and development awards from being made for any proposal in which a covered individual is participating in a malign foreign talent recruitment program; and

to the extent practicable, require recipient institutions to prohibit covered individuals participating in malign foreign talent recruitment programs from working on projects supported by research and development awards.

children.heading: Definition of foreign talent recruitment programs
children.label: c Definition of foreign talent recruitment programs
children.text: As part of the guidance under subsection (b), the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy shall define and describe the characteristics of a foreign talent recruitment program.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: d Implementation
children.text: Not later than one year after , each Federal research agency shall issue a policy utilizing the guidelines under subsection (b).August 9, 2022

children.heading: Consistency
children.label: e Consistency
children.text: The Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy shall ensure that the policies issued by the Federal research agencies under subsection (d) are consistent to the greatest extent practicable.
